items.title: Origin Energy confirms customer data breached in hack

items.title: The US-Saudi nuclear deal goes much further than some expected

children.content: At the end of February, the US administration and the Iranian regime were taking part in nuclear talks when Washington walked away from the negotiating table and launched a bombing campaign on Iran with Israel's Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu.

items.title: The former cocaine-dealing Sydney socialite linked to a far-right news site

children.content: A "right of centre" website claiming to be news, with more than 42,000 Facebook followers, may not be as independent as it says. An ABC NEWS Verify investigation has found the site uses AI and a fake news editor.

items.title: Bystanders praised for courage to stop attempted murder of woman

items.title: Researcher shocked as orca blows up a sunfish in front of her

children.content: Underwater video captures the moment killer whales in the Gulf of California slam into a sunfish, causing it to explode.
Researchers say the orcas may use the technique to make it easier for juvenile killer whales to feed or they do it as a fun group bonding exercise.

items.title: Insect repellent release leaves 11 staff sick at major SA hospital

children.content: Eleven staff at an Adelaide hospital have become unwell and a dozen emergency beds have been closed, after the accidental release of an "outdoor insect repellent" inside the facility.

children.content: The owner of one of Victoria's worst illegal dump sites has been handed a record fine of $60,000 and ordered to pay an expected $1 million to clean up the polluted property in Melbourne's outer north-east.
